entry.title: Re: [SAC Planning] Is it realistic to operate a “Category...
entry.content:
@79 

Performance is subjective and also depends on tenant sizing. 5000 to 50000 members can run fine if the tenant has sufficient memory and CPU allocations. Significant volume growth with inadequate tenant sizing can choke the overall performance.

You should think about the below considerations.

  • How many of the 50000 members will be actually booked.
  • Will the data volume grow while moving from # category to new category keys. Is the record splitting from 1:N
  • You will also have to think about rolling the members of this new category dimension into appropriate hierarchy for better scoping in stories and data action.
  • If rows displayed in stories are only 100, then is this the aggregated view or most category members are unbooked.

If its all about data analysis by a new category dimension which is concatenation of 3 existing attributes, you could try a calculated dimension in the story using the 3 existing attributes and it will provide you the category dimension for analysis.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Norway: Auto population of Payment Reference in Billi...
entry.content:
Note this response is from SAP  Joule- J4C

 

Auto-Populating Payment Reference Field (KIDNO) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Current State Analysis

The standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud functionality has significant limitations for auto-generating payment reference numbers (KIDNO) in outgoing invoices. The Payment Reference field (BSEG-KIDNO) is a standard system field that is not exposed for substitution or extensibility through available frameworks like BAdI FIN_ACDOC_ITEM_SUBSTITUTION or Manage Substitution and Validation Rules [1]. This creates a critical gap for Norwegian business requirements where KID numbers (kundeidentifikasjon) are essential for electronic payment processing and bank statement reconciliation.

Available Solutions

Option 1: Contract Accounting Module Approach

For organizations using Contract Accounting, SAP provides built-in payment reference generation capabilities:

  • Configure payment reference models through the "Maintain Payment Reference Activation" activity (ID: 106948) [2]
  • Activate payment reference generation by assigning your company code to the Contract Accounting application
  • This automatically generates payment references when posting outgoing invoices in the Contract Accounting module
Option 2: Convergent Invoicing for Nordic Countries

SAP has specific functionality for Nordic countries within Convergent Invoicing:

  • Outgoing CI invoices automatically generate and display reference numbers to comply with government regulations [3]
  • Payment reference logic varies based on payment format:
    • SEPA Direct Debit: Uses Official Document Number (ODN) if available, otherwise invoice number
    • Direct Debit with mandate: Uses ODN if available, otherwise invoice number
    • Direct Debit without mandate: Uses FIK number [3]
Option 3: Document Information Extraction Enhancement

Recent SAP enhancements include payment reference extraction capabilities:

  • Document Information Extraction service (OCR) now extracts Payment Reference fields from invoices [4]
  • This feature is automatically enabled and supports Nordic countries' requirements
  • Useful for inbound invoice processing but may not directly address outgoing invoice generation
Option 4: Custom Reference Fields Integration

For payment processing, you can include custom reference fields in payment medium formats:

  • Map custom fields to payment format structures [5]
  • Implement custom logic for payment reference handling
  • This approach focuses on payment file generation rather than invoice creation

Implementation Steps

  1. Requirement Validation: Confirm whether Contract Accounting or Convergent Invoicing modules are available in your template
  2. Module Assessment: Evaluate if your invoice processes can leverage Contract Accounting functionality
  3. Configuration Setup: If applicable, configure payment reference models and activate generation for your Norwegian company code
  4. Testing Phase: Validate payment reference generation in test invoices and verify bank statement reconciliation
  5. Template Rollout: Deploy configuration across Norwegian entities and extend to Sweden/Finland as needed

Technical Considerations

  • SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud restricts direct field substitution for BSEG-KIDNO to maintain financial posting consistency [1]
  • Contract Accounting module provides the most viable standard solution for payment reference auto-generation [2]
  • Convergent Invoicing offers country-specific functionality but may require module licensing [3]
  • Any custom development would require feature requests through SAP Customer Influence Portal [1]

Risks and Mitigation

  • Module Dependency: Solution effectiveness depends on available SAP modules in your license
  • Template Compatibility: Ensure chosen approach aligns with your global template design
  • Upgrade Impact: Standard SAP functionality ensures future upgrade compatibility
  • Fallback Option: Manual payment reference entry may be required if standard solutions are unavailable

Next Steps

  1. Immediate Assessment: Verify if Contract Accounting or Convergent Invoicing modules are included in your SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud license
  2. Stakeholder Alignment: Discuss module-based solutions with your project team and business stakeholders
  3. Feature Request: If standard solutions are insufficient, submit enhancement requests via SAP Customer Influence Portal [1]
  4. Nordic Rollout Planning: Coordinate implementation approach across Norway, Sweden, and Finland requirements

Would you like me to help you assess which SAP modules are available in your current license, or would you prefer guidance on configuring the Contract Accounting payment reference functionality?

entry.title: Re: Data Actions on Formula Account Fails in DEV but work...

@Sorro 

Copy Action with source members having formula generally has a performance impact and it could be that the dev tenant sizing capacity is not enough or is getting throttled. You could check the details of the error in System> Overview to see if more details are available to ascertain the root cause. You might find something in Alerts section or Error and Warning section of the overview.

Hope this helps!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration – Cloud based ERP Synchronization of Technical Objects
entry.content: Good question @JOSE-ODAR. BNAC itself is not a mobility application and does not require a separate mobile link. However, the integration between BNAC and ERP currently relies on the MAIF (Mobile Add-on Integration Framework) components available in the ERP backend.

entry.title: Re: SAC Calendar time to complete actions in Parallel
entry.content:
I am not aware of any general systemic reason why running two multi-actions or data actions in parallel should take significantly more time then running the same sequentially.

Generally the only constraints would be the number of virtual CPU and memory of your tenant, so unless you are at near capacity due to high activity this should not have significant impact.

There could be a bandwith issue if you are running parallel data actions that read from or write to an external system (live data models or writing to S/4HANA for instance, but we would need to analyze your system and process to determine if any of the above are the case. For this issue I would suggest you open a support incident with SAP so that we can investigate this further.

entry.title: How do Customizing Tenant and Development Tenant work together in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently preparing for my Sourcing and Procurement  certification and studying the system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. I have a question regarding the two workspaces in SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) and how they relate to the overall landscape.

In CBC, when selecting the Deployment Target, I can see two available targets:
- SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape — Training Customizing (Entitlement Role: Training Customizing)
- SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape — Training Development (Entitlement Role: Training Development)

 

From what I understand:
- One CBC workspace is connected to the Customizing Tenant
- A second CBC workspace is connected to the Development Tenant
- Both are part of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud development system

My background: I have worked on SAP Private Cloud / On-Premise projects where we had a classic 4-system landscape (D, Q, R, P) with customizing and development handled together in the D system and transported in one direction through the landscape.

My questions are:

1. Do the Customizing Tenant and Development Tenant each have their own independent transport landscape (e.g. each with their own Q, R and P systems)? Or do they share the same downstream systems?

2. How do developments or extensions built in the Development Tenant eventually reach the production environment? Is there a transport mechanism between the two tenants at any point?

3. Where do business users perform UAT — only in the Customizing Tenant, or also in the Development Tenant? If both tenants need to be tested, does this mean UAT must be conducted twice?

Thank you very much for any clarification!

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Clarification on data monitor tasks when running cons...
entry.content:
Hello Manas,

Well the answer is simple: the consolidation for the last period of the year is the consolidation for the year. The basis for consolidation is YTD data (ACDOCU stores YTD data). If they don't want monthly or periodic consolidation, the global parameters for the last period and fiscal year will give the yearly consolidation. We don't need to run the data monitor or consolidation tasks for the last but one periods at all. In the reports please select YTD instead of Periodic to see the correct result for the full year consolidation.  

Regards.

entry.title: 財務リーダーが価格戦略で「ゲームを変える」時代 ―― SAP CPQが切り拓く企業価値向上の可能性
entry.content:
はじめに：価格設定はなぜ経営課題なのか

B2B企業において、価格の設定方法は企業価値（株価）に対して深い影響を及ぼします。それは単に「製品にいくら付けるか」という問題ではなく、組織全体の価格計算に用いるメソドロジー（方法論）そのものが問題の本質です。

価格設定メソドロジーは、営業・マーケティング・財務の各部門をつなぐ共通言語として組織に深く根付いています。だからこそ、それが「制度化」され、「ずっとこのやり方でやってきた」という固定観念として、競争優位のための変革が妨げられるケースが後を絶ちません。

本稿では、価格設定メソドロジーがいかに企業価値に影響を与え得るか、そしてそれをどのように刷新することで大きな価値創出が可能かを、具体的な事例とともに解説します。財務リーダーの方々には特に、この「見落とされがちな競争優位の源泉」に注目していただきたいと思います。

価格の種類とメソドロジー：「価格」という言葉の多様性を理解する

「価格」という言葉は多様な意味を持ちます。B2B取引においては、以下のような種類が存在します。

  • 定価（List Price）：企業が公表する標準的な販売価格。ディスカウントの起点として機能します。
  • 実売価格（Street Price）：顧客が実際に支払う平均的な価格。定価からの典型的な割引額を差し引いた水準です。
  • フロア価格（Floor Price）：営業担当者が上位承認なしに提示できる最低価格。
  • 取引価格（Transaction Price）：特定の顧客が特定の取引で実際に支払った価格。ERPや財務システムに記録されます。
  • 価格リスト（Price Lists）：業種別・顧客別など複数の価格体系。システムは優先順位に従い最適な価格を自動判定する必要があります。

価格設定の方法論としても、コストプラス価格法・競合価格法・バリューベース価格法・ダイナミックプライシング・マージンベース価格法・地域/チャネル別価格法など多岐にわたります。一つの見積書の中で複数のメソドロジーが混在するケースも少なくありません。

重要な点は、ほとんどのB2B企業は長年同じ価格メソドロジーを使い続けており、変更できると考えてすらいないという現実です。しかし、価格メソドロジーの刷新こそが、競争優位を生む大きな機会となり得ます。

価格設定を「営業ツール」として活用する

調査によると、正確で完全な見積書を最初に提示した売り手が、取引のほとんどを勝ち取ることが示されています。これは「アンカリング効果」と呼ばれる認知バイアスに基づくもので、意思決定において最初に提示された情報が強い影響力を持つためです。

また、顧客はより簡単で快適な購買体験を求めています。かつてBlockbusterでビデオをレンタルする際に延滞料のストレスを感じていたように、複雑な価格体系や煩雑な見積プロセスはB2B顧客を遠ざけます。Netflixが延滞料なしのサービスでゲームを変えたように、シンプルで分かりやすい価格体系が受注率の向上に直結します。

さらに、価格設定は成約タイミングのコントロールにも活用できます。期限付き割引による早期クロージングの誘導、あるいは将来購買へのインセンティブ付与による収益の平準化など、営業戦略と価格設定は密接に連動します。

効率的な見積プロセスは、営業担当者が同期間内により多くの見積を作成することを可能にし、販売コストの削減につながります。 B2B企業では通常、見積の約半数から三分の一しか受注に結び付かないため、見積作成の効率化は収益性改善において非常に重要です。

価格設定を「マーケティングツール」として活用する

価格は、Webサイトや広告などマーケティング活動における価値訴求の手段にもなります。「5部屋一律〇〇円」のような分かりやすい価格設定は、プロモーションで活用しやすく、受注率向上に寄与します。

また、Progressive Insuranceが販売チャネルによって価格を変えているように、価格設計によって顧客を特定のチャネルに誘導することも可能です。逆にAppleは全チャネルで統一価格を維持し、顧客に「必ず最良の価格で購入できる」という安心感を与えています。

選択肢の数も重要な要素です。 選択肢が少なすぎると顧客は制限を感じ、多すぎると「選択オーバーロード」が発生します。自動車メーカーが「トリムレベル（グレード別パッケージ）」を設定することで顧客の選択を簡略化しているように、B2B見積においても提示するオプション数を適切にコントロールすることが購買意思決定を促進します。

価格設定を「財務ツール」として活用する ―― CFOへのメッセージ

財務部門がとりわけ注目すべき点は、価格設定が企業の収益性・財務リスク管理・企業価値に直接影響することです。

1. 利益率に基づくディスカウント管理
CPQ（Configure, Price, Quote）ソフトウェアは、製品コストを営業担当者に開示することなく、見積の利益率の健全性を「赤・黄・緑」のインジケーターで可視化できます。これにより、機密情報を守りながら、全社的に利益率を意識した値引き管理が実現します。

2. 為替リスクの軽減
海外市場に販売する企業は為替変動リスクに直面します。CPQソフトウェアは、入金予定日に基づいて見積価格と通貨を動的に調整し、為替ヘッジ戦略と連携した価格管理を実現します。

3. 製品設定ミスによる損失（Write-down）の防止
製造不可能な構成で誤って見積もりを提示してしまった場合、追加コストや値引きで対応せざるを得ず、その分が直接利益を圧迫します。CPQの製品コンフィギュレーター機能により、製造可能な組み合わせのみが見積可能となり、こうした損失を事前に防止できます。

4. M&Aシナジーの早期実現
企業買収後、統合製品ラインのバンドル構成と価格設定を迅速に行うことで、投資家に約束した「買収の効果実現スピード」を高めることができます。CPQはこのプロセスを大幅に加速します。

5. 規制対応と正確な売上予測
販売可能な地域・対象顧客の自動チェックや見積書への必要記載事項の自動挿入により、コンプライアンスリスクを低減します。また、見積更新のたびにCRMの商談情報が自動更新されることで、財務アナリストが重視する売上予測の精度向上にも貢献します。上場企業にとって、予測精度は株式評価にも影響を与える重要な指標です。

まとめ：CPQは「価格革新」を実現するインフラ

Blockbusterはその商品価格でNetflixに負けたわけではありません。価格のメソドロジーと体験設計で敗れたのです。同様に、B2B企業においても、製品力だけでなく価格戦略の高度化が競争優位を生む時代が来ています。

多くの企業がより洗練された価格戦略を採用しない理由は、「メールとスプレッドシートでは実装できないから」です。SAP CPQはまさにそのギャップを埋めるソリューションとして設計されており、コストプラスからバリューベース、ダイナミックプライシングに至る多様な価格メソドロジーを実装可能にします。

財務リーダーにとって、価格設定メソドロジーの刷新は、大きな企業価値向上の機会です。 今こそ、「ずっとこのやり方でやってきた」という固定観念を見直し、CPQを活用した価格戦略の変革を検討してみてください。

著者：Bill Butler（SAP CPQ グローバルプロダクトマーケティングマネージャー）

原文はこちらでご確認ください。
SAP CPQソリューションの詳細は、SAPの公式サイトをご覧ください。

entry.title: From Intelligent to Autonomous: What Actually Changed at SAP Sapphire 2026
entry.content:
From Intelligent to Autonomous: What Actually Changed at SAP Sapphire 2026

I'll be honest... when I first saw the Sapphire 2026 keynote slides, I rolled my eyes a little.

No SAP keynote is complete without a rebrand, and this one delivered right on schedule. We've gone from the Intelligent Enterprise to the Autonomous Enterprise in roughly the time it takes most customers to finish an S/4HANA migration. Same vendor, fresh adjective, bigger marketing budget.

That was my first reaction. New label, same platform.

Then I actually went deeper. Sat with it for a bit. And I changed my mind.

There's a real shift happening here, and most of the reaction pieces I've seen so far are missing it. So let me try to walk through what I think actually changed... past the keynote choreography and the partner logos.

Where We've Been: BTP at the Bottom

For most of the last decade, the story was the Intelligent Enterprise.

The pitch was simple enough. Break the silos, connect the data, embed a bit of AI as a feature, and you'd end up with a business that runs on real-time insight. Decisions get better because the information underneath them gets better.

And underneath all of it sat one platform. SAP BTP.

That's the mental model most of us built our careers on. Every architecture diagram, every slide, every solution conversation  start with BTP at the base, work upward. Integration sits here. Extensions sit here. Data, Analytics, Automation sit here. AI capabilities sit here. BTP was the floor.

The Intelligent Enterprise gave the human better data. The human still made the call.

What Changed: The Bet Is Different Now

Sapphire 2026 is SAP making a different bet entirely.

Christian's framing on stage didn't leave a lot of room for interpretation. The era of "almost right" is over, he said. Mission-critical processes need AI that doesn't just suggest the answer... it carries the work through, within the same controls a human would have to operate within.

That's not a tweak. That's a posture change.

The Intelligent Enterprise told you what to do. The Autonomous Enterprise just does it, with you supervising the strategy and the agents handling the execution. Whether you think that's exciting or terrifying probably depends on how many AP automation projects you've personally tried to drag across the finish line.

But honestly, the philosophy isn't the part that moved me. The architecture is.

The Foundation Has Quietly Moved

Read SAP's own press release carefully. There's a line in it that's easy to miss:

"SAP Business AI Platform is a new foundation for building and deploying enterprise AI."

A new foundation.

So what is it? It's BTP, Business Data Cloud, and Business AI (along with BTM solutions like LeanIX) — all three bundled up under one roof with shared governance. Three things we used to talk about separately, now stitched together and called something else.

Read that twice, because it matters.

BTP hasn't gone anywhere. It's still there. But it's no longer the foundation... it's a component inside the new foundation. The platform we've spent years orienting ourselves around just got absorbed upward.

Here's how I'd draw it:

 

  Intelligent Enterprise Autonomous Enterprise
FoundationSAP BTPSAP Business AI Platform
What it does

Apps, Automation, Integration, Data & Analytics AI

Unifies BTP + BDC + Business AI (along with Business Transformation Solutions)
AI's roleEmbedded featureThe operating layer
Human roleInformed decision-makerStrategic supervisor
VisionInsight-driven decisionsSelf-executing processes

If you design solutions on SAP, you're going to have to update your mental model. The base of the diagram just changed.

Why I Stopped Calling It a Rebrand

The thing that finally pushed me past the cynicism was a single sentence from Christian during the keynote:

"No AI agent can compensate for a bad data landscape."

I'd encourage you to read that one twice as well.

The CEO of SAP. On the Sapphire main stage. With every partner logo lit up behind him. Saying out loud what those of us who've spent careers in master data and BTP have been muttering at customers for years.

That's not a marketing line. That's a positioning statement, and it tells you exactly what SAP is now betting the platform on.

And the numbers behind it back the bet up. 224 agents and 51 assistants already live across four business domains. A unified data foundation in Business Data Cloud. An AI Agent Hub to govern both SAP and non-SAP agents. Joule Studio 2.0 free, the runtime free through the end of 2026. Hybrid landscape support that reverses three years of cloud-only positioning — and if you've sat in a single influencing meeting recently, you'll know that one is a much bigger deal than the press is making it 😉

This isn't a slide deck. It's an integrated bet that the layers that win the agentic era are data, governance, and orchestration. Not the models. Not the assistants. The plumbing underneath.

So What Do You Actually Do With This?

A few honest thoughts, one practitioner to another.

Your platform conversations need to update. "We'll do this on BTP" is no longer a complete answer. BTP is a component now. The full conversation includes Business Data Cloud and the Business AI layer alongside it. Architects, this means thinking in three layers, not one — and a few of your slide decks are going to need a refresh.

Data foundations stop being optional. If you've been treating master data governance as a nice-to-have, or as something you'll get to in Phase Two after the go-live settles down, the Autonomous Enterprise will quietly punish that decision. Agents fail on bad data faster and more visibly than humans do. Christian said as much himself.

Clean core just got teeth. A lot of the AI scenarios announced assume clean, governed core data and standard SAP processes. Custom code makes agentic execution measurably harder. If you've been struggling to win the clean core argument internally, you've just got fresh ammunition.

One Last Thought

Yes, SAP rebranded. They always do. I fully expect at least one more brand layer before this decade is done.

But this time the rebrand sits on top of something real. A unified platform stack. Hundreds of shipped agents. A CEO who said out loud what every honest data person in the SAP ecosystem has been saying for years... AI is only ever as good as the data sitting underneath it.

The Intelligent Enterprise told us we needed better information.

The Autonomous Enterprise is telling us we need a better foundation.

More blog posts coming soon!

entry.title: Connect SAP S/4HANA Odata Service Directly with SAP APIM with Oauth2.0 Based Authentication
entry.content:
Direct integration between SAP S/4HANA OData Services and SAP API Management using OAuth 2.0 enables secure and efficient API communication without routing through SAP CPI. This approach helps organizations simplify their architecture while maintaining centralized API security and governance.

In this blog, we will cover how to configure SAP API Management to securely expose SAP S/4HANA OData services using OAuth 2.0 authentication.


Prerequisites

  • SAP Integration Suite with API Management capability enabled
  • SAP Cloud Connector configured
  • SAP S/4HANA OData service activate

I am assuming that you have enabled API Management capability in Integration Suite.

Step 1 : Create API Provider

Connection

Type

On Premise

Host

Virtual Host, as mentioned in Cloud Connector

Port

Virtual Port, as mentioned in Cloud Connector

Location ID

As mentioned in Cloud Connector

Authentication

NONE

Additional Properties:

sap-client: XXX

Catalog Service Settings

Path Prefix

/sap/opu/odata

Service Collection URL

/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/ServiceCollection

Authentication type

BASIC

Username

SAP's User Name for communication

Password

***

After Configuring the above you can perform Test Connection I have also Attached screenshot for your reference.

Step 2 :  Create Key Value Map to store the credentials to S/4HANA(Click on Encrypted)

Step 3 : Click on Create API . Select the API Provider that we have configured Earlier and Click on Discover.

Step 4 : After Clicking on Discover select the Name and click on ok. Once you click ok then proxy will be created . 

Step 5 : Create a Subscription for the Product created.

Step 6 : The Go the the Policies tab.  In Proxyendpoint in PreFlow apply OAuthv2 Policy.
For Oauth2 Authentication setup in detail  you are refer my below blog
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/configuring-oauth-2-0-authentication-between-sap-api-management-and-sap/qaq-p/14275375#M4932247 

Oauth Policy

<OAuthV2 async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
   <ExternalAuthorization>false</ExternalAuthorization>
   <Operation>VerifyAccessToken</Operation>
   <GenerateResponse enabled="true"/>
   <Tokens/>
</OAuthV2>

In proxyEndpoint Postflow I have implemented AssignMessage Policy.

Assign Message – If you encounter the error {"fault":{"faultstring":"Unsupported Encoding \"br\"","detail":{"errorcode":"protocol.http.UnsupportedEncoding"}}}, it's because BR (Brotli) is a compression format typically used by backends for webpage loading, and it is not supported here. Using the Assign Message policy helps remove this unsupported encoding so the message can reach the target successfully.

<AssignMessage async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
    <Remove>
        <Headers>
            <Header name="Authorization"/>
        </Headers>
    </Remove>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>false</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
    <AssignTo createNew="false" type="request">request</AssignTo>
</AssignMessage>

Step 7 : In Target Endpoint in Postflow I have added Key value map policy and Basic Auth Policy.

Policy Key Value Map 

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="s4hana" async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <Get assignTo="private.basic_user" index="1">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>s4user</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Get assignTo="private.basic_password" index="1">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>s4password</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Scope>environment</Scope>
</KeyValueMapOperations>

Step 8  : Apply Basic Auth Policy.

Basic Auth 

<BasicAuthentication async='true' continueOnError='false' enabled='true' xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
    <Operation>Encode</Operation>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
    <User ref='private.basic_user'></User>
    <Password ref='private.basic_password'></Password>
    <AssignTo createNew="false">request.header.Authorization</AssignTo>
</BasicAuthentication>

Step 9 : Click on Deploy

Step 10 : Time to test.  

Select authentication as Oauth2 and get the token.

Endpoint:  The Proxy Endpoint

GET /ProductMasterSet?$filter=FilterDate eq '20251023'

Description: Retrieves product master data filtered by the specified date.

Request Example

GET https://xyz-apim.test.apimanagement.in30.hana.ondemand.com:443/ProductMasterSet?$filter=FilterD… eq '20251023'

Headers: Accept: application/json Authorization: Bearer <access_token>

Conclusion

In this blog, we explored how to directly integrate SAP S/4HANA OData Services with SAP API Management using OAuth 2.0 authentication. We covered API Provider configuration, OAuth policy setup, Key Value Map implementation, and secure API exposure.

Using this approach, organizations can simplify integrations, centralize API security, and securely expose SAP S/4HANA services without routing requests through SAP CPI.

Reference
https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/create-api-provider
https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/setting-up-oauth-for-cloud-integration-in-cloud-foundry
https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/create-key-value-map

entry.title: Intelligent Error Handling in SAP CPI Using AI Adapter
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) provides powerful integration capabilities, but analyzing runtime exceptions and identifying resolutions often requires manual effort. With the new AI Adapter in SAP Integration Suite, we can now build intelligent error handling flows that automatically analyze exceptions using Large Language Models (LLMs) and return meaningful resolutions back to the source system.

In this blog, we will build an AI-powered error handling mechanism where:

  • CPI captures runtime exceptions
  • Sends the error text to an AI model using the AI Adapter
  • AI analyzes the issue
  • Returns Cause and Resolution
  • CPI sends the intelligent response back to the sender

 

Traditionally, when an iFlow fails, support teams manually analyze:

    • MPL logs
    • Exception stack traces
    • Adapter errors
    • HTTP failures

This process can be time-consuming for support teams and integration developers.

Using AI, we can automate:

      • Error classification
      • Root cause analysis
      • Suggested resolution
      • Troubleshooting guidance

This helps reduce support effort and improves response time.

Architecture Overview

Flow:

Sender → Exception Subprocess → AI Adapter → AI Model → Response Back to Sender

The Exception Subprocess captures the CPI runtime error and forwards the error text to an LLM through the AI Adapter.

The AI model then generates:

        • Error Summary
        • Root Cause
        • Resolution Steps

 

Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure the following:

          • SAP Integration Suite tenant
          • AI Adapter available in CPI
          • API Key for OpenAI/OpenRouter
          • AI Receiver Adapter configured

In this example, we use OpenRouter free models for testing purposes.

Step 1: Create Security Material

Navigate to:

Monitor → Security Material

Create:

  • Type: Secure Parameter
  • Name: OPENROUTER_API_KEY

Value:

  • Bearer sk-or-v1-xxxxxxxx

 

Step 2: Configure AI Adapter

Add an AI Receiver Adapter inside the Exception Subprocess.

Adapter Configuration

 

Address

OpenRouter URL/OpenAI URL

Resource Path

/api/v1/chat/completions

HTTP Method

POST

Authentication

API Key Alias- Created in Step 1

 

 

 

 

 

Step 3: Configure Request Body

Use the following JSON payload:

{
  "model": "openai/gpt-4",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are an SAP CPI support expert."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Analyze this SAP CPI error and provide Error, Cause, and Resolution: ${property.CamelExceptionCaught}"
    }
  ],
  "temperature": 0.3,
  "max_tokens": 400
}

 

 

Understanding the Roles

system Role

Defines AI behavior.

Example:

{
  "role": "system",
  "content": "You are an SAP CPI support expert."
}

This helps the model provide SAP-specific troubleshooting responses.

user Role

Contains the actual exception text from CPI.

Example:

{
  "role": "user",
  "content": "Analyze this SAP CPI error..."
}

 

Step 4: Capture Exception in Exception Subprocess

Inside the Exception Subprocess:

  • Add Content Modifier
  • Store exception into property:

                    ${exception.message} or ${property.CamelExceptionCaught}

  This property is then passed to the AI Adapter.

Step 5: Return AI Response to Sender

After AI Adapter execution:

  • Use Content Modifier or Groovy Script
  • Extract AI response
  • Return formatted response to sender

Example output:

Error:
Invalid endpoint configuration

Cause:
The HTTP receiver adapter contains an incorrect URL path.

Resolution:
Verify the endpoint path and ensure the API resource URL is valid.

 

 

 

 

Sample Use Cases

This approach can be used for:

    • HTTP adapter failures
    • SOAP faults
    • OData authentication errors
    • Mapping exceptions
    • XML parsing issues
    • API throttling analysis
    • Runtime deployment failures

 

Benefits of AI-Based Error Handling

Faster Troubleshooting

Developers receive instant suggestions without manually checking logs.

Reduced Support Effort

Support teams can automate first-level analysis.

Improved Developer Productivity

AI provides contextual recommendations and possible fixes.

Better Monitoring Experience

Instead of cryptic stack traces, users receive readable explanations.

entry.title: Re: Composable Storefront local setup and OAuth Framework Update JDK21
entry.content:
Hi @kommalapati,

In multiple websites context, you need to declare distinct OAuth client for each domain name / website. Regarding OCC API aspect, you should use CDN capability upfront to forward public domain name request to azure domain name OCC API service. 

Remember is strongly recommended to use CDN when you activate headless architecture. One reason is that the SSR should never be directly requested by the end user's browser (SSR cannot support heavy workload).

At the end from browser perspective there's no cross-site requests. The front-end application download (first hit / SSR) and API calls use the same domain name (CSR logic).

Finally, you just apply one of security principles by hiding your backend / infrastructure logic where from browser point of view everything is accessible through one endpoint / one domain name.

entry.title: New ways in S4HANA to enhance or add input fields in the Transportation Cockpit using Field Catalog
entry.content:
Introduction:

In the SAP S/4HANA 2023 release for SAP TM onwards, new functionality is now available: Field Catalogs for the Transportation Cockpit. Using this feature, you can add custom fields, bring standard fields to the cockpit, hide/unhide fields, change properties, and set up a variant for the Transportation Cockpit(TC), setting up a sequence of which fields will be displayed. This feature does not require any enhancements to the Web Dynpro Component Configuration. 

This blog will illustrate all the configurations and steps on how to add editable custom fields to the Transportation Cockpit and save data to the TOR root.

Explanation:

First, the custom fields were added to the TOR Root Note Extension Structure - /SCMTMS/INCL_EEW_TOR_ROOT

Then go to Fiori Tile in your development/customizing client 

In the Define Field Catalogs for TC tile, you will see all standard field catalogs available for TC. Usually, we copy the standard field catalog to the Z custom based on the requirement and make configuration changes to the Z custom catalog. Once changes are done, the same Z custom field catalog is configured in Page Layouts for the Transportation Cockpit Tile. 

In our case, I am using Road Freight Orders, Content object = 0033, Z field catalog created. 

Navigating to the details in the Field Catalog, the screen below is displayed, showing the generated maximum structure, the user interface structure, and all available fields 

Now you see the Maximum User Interface Structure in this case - /SCMTMS/S_TC_M_0033. And the same zz custom fields were already available in this structure, as had been added to the append structure TOR extension include earlier.

Refresh the field catalog page you will see these custom fields in the section below:

Check the Display (Default) for the custom fields and sequence accordingly, and save. It will add the fields to the TC. 

Make sure the Z field catalog is mapped to page layouts as below:

TC displaying with custom fields:

If the custom fields are non-editable and for display only purposes, no further changes are required; the following steps can be ignored.

To make the custom fields editable in the TC. Below, the Class method was implemented. 

Class - /SCMTMS/CL_UI_PLN_UIBB_BASE

Method - GET_ENHANCEMENT_FIELD_DESCR

In the RETURNING structure of this method for each custom field, the display type was set to input field display_type   = sc_enhancement_display_types-input_field 

A bit of debugging will help for sure. And you will get all the custom fields in this method in the IMPORT structure to change the field display properties.

Once you launch the TC now, the custom fields will be input and editable. 

Next Step: data saving and setting the UI controller TAG to identify a change in the editable field

Since it was a Road Freight Order List in TC, the following class and method were enhanced.

/SCMTMS/CL_UI_VIEWEXIT_PLN_NEW

Method - /BOFU/IF_FBI_VIEW_EXITINTF_RUN~ADAPT_CHANGE_LOG

CT_CHANGE_LOG below will have all the new values and old values of the fields changed in the TC.

For data passing to the TOR mass structure for the custom fields, the structure below was enhanced. Also, the same field name was added, and the _CHGD flag field was added for each custom field. This is mandatory. 

Once the above structure is enhanced, in the method - /BOFU/IF_FBI_VIEW_EXITINTF_RUN~ADAPT_CHANGE_LOG, we need to populate the lt_tor_massupd structure with the input data for the custom field and set 'X' for the _CHGD field as below. 

Then set the UI controller TAG to identify a change in an editable field;

Now all the steps are completed. Test the changes; it should work seamlessly. 

Conclusion:

With this approach, there were no changes made in the WDA/FPM component. Using the same approach, other Transportation Cockpit Lists and Hierarchies can be extended/enhanced.

I hope this blog information is helpful. Please drop a comment for any feedback/suggestions/questions.

entry.title: Re: HANA NSE Buffersize per Server in Scale Out Environment
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

When both max_size and max_size_rel are set, the system will use the smaller value.

When these values are applied to a scale-out system at the DATABASE layer, max_size_rel will be relative to the allocation for each host.

For example, if the max_size_rel is equal to 10 percent of the allocation limit for the given host, but it is capped at a max_size of 20 gigabytes, the relative setting (max_size_rel) is used for smaller hosts, whereas the absolute cap (max_size) is used for larger hosts.

Please Refer the Online document "Adjusting the Buffer Cache Size": 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/2436bae042054ec880ff5d1f436ef0a0.html 

 

~Gaurav

entry.title: Planning your transition paths to SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Every architecture conversation with customers ends up at the same point: how do we move without disruption, and still create room for new ideas?

This SAP Partners session focuses on transition paths to SAP Business Data Cloud, and the options you can bring into those discussions right away.

We will explore greenfield and migration approaches, and what each means in real customer situations.  You will also see how SAP HANA Cloud and SAP BDC connect through bi-directional data exchange, helping hybrid landscapes stay connected instead of split.

Plus, we will look at how SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Databricks fit into the same picture, so data products feel connected across the stack.

Honestly, the thing is, most projects do not follow a single clean path.  This session helps you explain those mixed realities in a simple way.

If you are shaping data architecture with customers, this is worth joining.

QuocDT5

entry.title: Leveraging AI for Predictive Data Quality in SAP S/4HANA Transformations
entry.content:
Leveraging AI for Predictive Data Quality in SAP S/4HANA Transformations

Introduction

One thing I’ve learned from running large S/4HANA programs is that data quality issues almost always show up later than anyone expects. Sometimes it’s during SIT, sometimes UAT, and occasionally right before cutover — the exact moment when nobody wants surprises. By then, even small inconsistencies can trigger downstream issues and create unnecessary pressure on both business and technical teams.

Traditional validation still matters, but it’s reactive. You only find the issue after the data has already moved through part of the migration process. That’s exactly where predictive analytics and AI‑driven techniques can make a real difference.

Key Takeaways

  • Traditional validation is necessary but often reactive in large S/4HANA programs.
  • AI helps uncover recurring patterns and subtle inconsistencies that manual checks usually miss.
  • Even simple anomaly detection models can provide early warning signals.
  • Embedding predictive checks into each migration cycle reduces late‑stage rework and improves consistency.
  • The biggest benefit of AI is earlier visibility, not automation — it helps teams make better decisions sooner.

Why Traditional Validation Isn’t Enough

Most programs rely on rule‑based checks, manual reconciliation, and post‑load reviews. These work for known issues, but they don’t help you spot patterns that aren’t obvious — especially when you’re dealing with multiple regions and large data volumes.

And when the same issues keep coming back cycle after cycle, traditional validation rarely explains why.

Where AI Actually Helps

AI can highlight patterns that humans simply don’t see:

  • recurring field failures
  • strange value distributions
  • duplicate or inconsistent master data
  • unusual transactional spikes
  • region‑specific data creation issues

And you don’t need a massive AI platform. Even simple anomaly detection can give you early warning signals that help teams focus on the right areas before they become expensive problems.

Illustrative example based on practical migration validation and anomaly detection scenarios observed during enterprise SAP S/4HANA transformation programs.

Anecdote 1: The “Random” Customer Failures That Weren’t Random

On one project, we kept fixing the same customer master issues cycle after cycle. Every time, a different set of customers failed — no obvious pattern. The business team insisted the data was clean because the same records worked fine in the legacy system.

Out of curiosity, I ran a simple anomaly detection check on the historical load logs. What stood out was that all failing customers were created during a specific two‑month period by one regional team. When we dug deeper, we found they were using an outdated template missing partner function defaults.

It wasn’t a technical issue — it was a process issue.
And without the predictive check, we would have kept firefighting symptoms instead of fixing the root cause.

Anecdote 2: The Transaction Spike That Saved Us Before UAT

Before UAT on another program, our predictive checks flagged a strange spike in a specific financial posting type. The business initially dismissed it as normal month‑end activity. But the model kept flagging it, so we investigated.

It turned out that a legacy interface had been quietly duplicating certain postings whenever the source system experienced latency. The issue had existed for years, but nobody noticed because the volume was small and spread across multiple company codes.

If we had discovered this during cutover, it would have caused a reconciliation nightmare.
Catching it early allowed us to correct the data and adjust the extraction logic before UAT even started.

Why Predictive Checks Should Be Part of Every Migration Cycle

Predictive checks work best when they’re integrated into the migration lifecycle — not treated as a separate “AI experiment.”

When embedded into each cycle, predictive analytics helps teams:

  • identify recurring issues earlier
  • improve consistency across regions
  • reduce dependency on manual reconciliation
  • minimize pressure during cutover
  • track data quality trends over time

It shifts the team from reactive cleanup to proactive prevention.

Conclusion

AI won’t replace validation teams. But it will help them focus on the right areas earlier — and in S/4HANA programs, early visibility is often the difference between a smooth cutover and a stressful one.

From my experience, the real value of AI in migration isn’t automation.
It’s better decision‑making, earlier insights, and fewer surprises during the phases where surprises hurt the most.

entry.title: Re: Customer-Managed Data Products in SAP BDC: What Really Happens When Your Source Is a Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

When you mention:

Transformation Flow to HDLF (Copy 2): A Transformation Flow reads the materialized HANA local table and writes the result as a Delta Lake file (Parquet and transaction log) into the Datasphere HDLF File Space. This is the second physical copy on the SAP side.

Could you tell me how it is possible? Transformation Flows works ONLY in one SPACE (source and target are in the same space) . Then, if you have the source in a physical HANA local Table in SPACE A (HANA) and the target Delta lake table in SPACE  B, how can you move data betweeen both spaces with a Transformation Flow?

entry.title: ABAP development under the Clean Core paradigm: From BAPIs to BO and CDS
entry.content:
Clean Core paradigm based on levels per object

In summer 2025 SAP announced its new Level Based Clean Core Approach  for ABAP development based on Levels which are used to classify all SAP standard objects and technologies: 

  • Level A – Released objects, cloud-ready, modern. Always the first choice
  • Level B – Classic APIs, Works today, but plan to move on. Limited cloud readiness or limited alignment with modern standards
  • Level C – Internal APIs, no cloud migration path. Only acceptable in clearly bounded  scenarios
  • Level D – No APIs, Avoid in any new design

In many typical custom developments in the past access to SAP standard objects was done by BAPI calls since BAPIs were recommended by SAP and considered as released and stable at least in ECC systems.

But when you look now in the S/4HANA Cloudification Repository  and you browse the BAPIs you will see as of today (17th May 26) for the component SAP Cloud ERP Private Latest:

  • 3847 BAPI* function modules
    • 23 (!) only released = Level A
    • 2361 Classic API = Level B
    • 122 not to be released = Level C
    • 441 No API = Level D

The biggest part are the Classic API BAPIs and the question is now how to go forward with SAP ABAP developments.

 

How to move from Classic APIs (Level B) to Released APIs (Level A, cloud ready) ?

The modern programming model in S/4HANA is ABAP RAP. When using the RAP model you can consume the RAP Business Object (assuming that it is released = Level A).

RAP BO interfaces can be consumed using the Entity Manipulation Language (EML).  Write and Action access can be achieved via its business object interface in the EML statement:

MODIFY ENTITIES OF My_Interface
				ENTITY BO_Entity
				CREATE …

Read Access can be done using the underlying CDS of the BO in a SELECT statement.

See a good example for Purchase Order here.

You will find the released BOs in the already mentioned Cloudification Repository 

Very helpful because of the structure of the methods and the object as well as ABAP EML code snippets is the Business Accelerator Hub.

A missing piece is the link from BAPI to RAP BO even it is obvious in many cases because the BAPI has the naming pattern BAPI_<BOR object>_<Method>.  The BOR object name is a semantic name (Business Partner) as is the RAP object name.

Nevertheless, it would be helpful that the successor information is added for the Classic APIs as well to the Coudification Repo as it is for many other not released objects.

 

A small example: Bank Master

The RAP BO for the Bank Master is described in the Business Accelerator Hub here.

If we compare the Bank Master BAPIs (already being created some time ago in SAP ECC) with the new BO we get the following:

BAPI (Level Classic APIs)

Corresponding BO replacement

Semantics

BAPI_BANK_CHANGE

 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp ENTITY bank UPDATE FIELDS …
MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp ENTITY bankaddress UPDATE FIELDS		Updates a Bank Master and its address

BAPI_BANK_CREATE

 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp ENTITY bank CREATE FIELDS …
MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp ENTITY bankaddress UPDATE FIELDS		Creates a Bank Master and its address

BAPI_BANK_GETDETAIL

 

SELECT SINGLE FROM i_banktp FIELDS …Reads a single Bank Master record

BAPI_BANK_GETLIST

 

SELECT FROM i_banktp FIELDS …Reads several Bank Master records

BAPI_BANK_SAVEREPLICA

 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp ENTITY bank UPDATE FIELDS … MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp ENTITY bankaddress UPDATE FIELDSUpdates/ or Creates a Bank Master and its address

<! No BAPI exists !>

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_banktp ENTITY bankaddress CREATE BY \_bankscriptvariant … FIELDS ( … ) WITH VALUE #( … %target = VALUE #( ( addressrepresentationcode = … )) )Create an international address version (e.q. Chinese)

For the international address version no BAPI was ever created by SAP in the past but it is now part of the RAP BO.

 

Summary: when is it necessary or recommended to move from Classic BAPIs to RAP BOs?

In case you want to be SAP Public Cloud ready, or you are already on Public Cloud where only Level A / released objects can be used you have no choice but to move to RAP BOs.

For the SAP Private Cloud / On Premise installations the old BAPIs of level B/ classic API will continue to run.

But there are some points to consider where a change from BAPI to BO is recommended:

  • The old BAPIs might not be enhanced further: If there is a new functionality or a new data segment for the concerning application, it might not become part of the BAPI. One example could be the draft enabling whose application progresses with each release in new S/4 Fiori applications and which is not supported in the BAPIs. Another more specific is the international address version in our Bank Master example above.

  • The validations and checks in the BAPI might be not 100% equal to the behaviour of the corresponding S/4HANA Fiori App: You might notice that the validations and checks of the RAP BO are developed newly and in parallel to the existing ones of the corresponding BAPI. You remember may be a similar situation in the past in ECC at the time when the BAPIs were introduced in parallel to an existing transaction and the error messages were slightly different …

  • New developments: Here it is recommended using modern ABAP, modern technologies and thus RAP BOs and trying to stay on Level A right from the beginning.

  • New possibilities in S/4HANA: For some objects there were never BAPIs introduced or the “BAPI coverage” of the objects was never complete. There might now be RAP BOs for that.
    An example is Measurement Document where developers were forced to write a Batch Input for the underlying transaction IK11 in the past because there was no BAPI available.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign 26.05 is now ready!
entry.content:
Hi @arunathakar ,

We introduced this feature to improve your experience by identifying terminated employees assigned as approvers in approval workflows. However, following its release, we discovered certain issues that required us to temporarily withdraw it in order to uphold the quality and reliability you expect from us.

We are fully committed to reintroducing this feature in a future release. To ensure it meets our highest standards, it will go through rigorous testing prior to relaunch, and we will communicate a confirmed timeline as soon as it is available.

All related documentation will be updated accordingly to reflect these changes.

entry.title: SAP Business AI Platform: #2 What Exactly It is and Its Components
entry.content:
In the previous blog, we explored the vision of the Autonomous Enterprise - A world where people set the direction…and AI executes.

The natural questions come next are:

  • So..what exactly is SAP Business AI Platform?
  • What are its core components?
  • Where do the AI models get business context?
  • How do AI agents access trusted data?
  • How are security, governance, and compliance maintained?

Let’s dive deep into these!

 

Disclaimer: This blog reflects my personal understanding, interpretation, and point of view. It is not an official publication or statement from SAP.

 

So… What Exactly is SAP Business AI Platform?

At SAP Sapphire 2026, SAP introduced SAP Business AI Platform as the foundation that brings together data, AI, and enterprise grade governance into one unified platform.

 

Let’s understand what it is and why its needed.

Today, customers are not looking for generic AI. They want AI that understands business intent, uses real business context and trusted data. They want AI that knows how business processes work and can take actions across applications. They also want enterprise grade governance and security. 

That means organizations need more than just AI models.

They need a platform that helps them:

  • Build and customize AI solutions
    • Create and orchestrate AI agents
    • Connect data, applications, and processes
    • Govern everything securely and responsibly
    • Scale AI across the entire enterprise

That platform is…SAP Business AI Platform.

In simple words…

SAP Business AI Platform is a unified platform that enables customers, partners, and SAP developers to build, extend, integrate, and govern AI assistants and AI agents across SAP and non-SAP landscapes.

If Autonomous Enterprise is the vision…

SAP Business AI Platform is the foundation that makes it possible.

 

SAP Business AI Platform is NOT a New Solution

SAP Business AI Platform is NOT a new solution but a comprehensive platform of existing solutions and platforms.

It brings together the capabilities of SAP BTP, AI Foundation, SAP BDC, SAP HANA Cloud, and some capabilities from the SAP BTM portfolio.

 

3 core components of SAP Business AI Platform

Build

One environment for building, integrating and running AI agents, applications, agent extensions, and workflows.

Two important components under Build are – Joule Studio and SAP Integration Suite.

Joule Studio

Joule Studio is the next generation of SAP Build offering and is SAP’s AI first development environment for building, and running agents, applications, and workflows.

  • It offers the most productive way to build and extend SAP AI solutions - from intent to requirements to deployment.
  • Joule Studio provides the flexibility of low/no-code and pro-code paths and embeds n8n for visual workflow composition.
  • With the Vercel integration developers can easily build Next.js based web applications using frameworks like React, and Angular complimenting SAP frameworks (UI5, Fiori).

 

SAP Integration Suite

SAP Integration Suite provides a trusted enterprise integration layer that connects AI agents, business processes, systems and, applications across SAP & non-SAP landscapes.

It also facilitates secure governance of enterprise API exposure, multi-LLM connectivity, and enterprise-wide agent executions.

SAP Integration Suite also enables agent connectivity with standard interaction with protocols like MCP and activates event-driven agentic workflows across enterprise systems and processes.

 

Contextualize & Reason

Generic AI can answer questions. But business AI needs to do much more.

SAP grounds AI in more than 50 years of encoded business process expertise. The combination of SAP Knowledge Graph and SAP’s own models is the key differentiator to go beyond the generic LLMs.

At the heart of this capability is Generative AI Hub, which provides access to SAP Domain Models alongside third-party LLMs, with a full suite of orchestration services — including prompt optimizer, benchmarking, evaluation, and data masking.

 

Few important components in this category are: 

 

SAP Knowledge Graph

Data by itself is just information. But businesses run on relationships.

A customer places an order. An order creates a delivery. A delivery triggers an invoice. An invoice impacts finance.

In other words, the relationship is clear:

Customer → Sales Order → Delivery → Invoice → Finance Posting

SAP Knowledge Graph connects all of these business objects and their relationships into a semantic network of meaning.

So… when an AI agent looks at an invoice, it can trace it back to the delivery, the original order, and the customer who initiated the transaction.

In simple words…

SAP Knowledge Graph helps AI understand how everything in your business is connected.

 

SAP Business Data Cloud

AI is only as good as the data it uses. If the data is incomplete, outdated, or inconsistent…the AI results will be unreliable. That is where SAP Business Data Cloud comes in.

 

SAP Business Data Cloud brings the data into a converged Data & Analytics solution spanning data warehousing, master data management, database, analytics, planning, and machine learning.

In simple words…

SAP Business Data Cloud gives AI access to complete, clean, contextual, and current business data.

 

SAP & Non-SAP Models

SAP Business AI Platform does not rely only on third party models from companies such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and Mistral AI.

SAP also provides its own business focused AI models. These models are trained using SAP’s deep understanding of business processes and industry knowledge.

SAP RPT-1 is designed specifically for structured business data. SAP-ABAP-1 is model is built specifically for ABAP development.

 

Govern

Building AI is only half the story.

The bigger questions are:

  • Can we trust it?
  • Is it secure and compliant?
  • Is it actually delivering business value?

SAP Business AI Platform is designed to address both challenges from day one.

Every agent, application, LLM, and MCP server operates within the boundaries the business defines from the moment it goes live.

Every AI initiative is continuously measured against the outcomes it was deployed to deliver.

 

In simple words…

You do not just deploy AI and hope for the best.

You define the rules. You monitor the outcomes. And you continuously verify that AI is creating real business value.

 

How are SAP Business AI Platform and SAP BTP related?

SAP Business AI Platform is SAP’s new comprehensive platform to build, extend, integrate, and govern AI assistants and AI agents across SAP and non-SAP landscapes.

SAP BTP capabilities are a core part of Business AI Platform.

SAP is now positioning SAP Business AI Platform as SAP’s platform, and position BTP’s capabilities as part of this platform.

entry.title: Re: Customer-Managed Data Products in SAP BDC: What Really Happens When Your Source Is a Datasphere
entry.content:
@VineetBakshi Thank you for detail write up and this clearly helps in understanding the under the hood of Zero Copy. It is true that Zero copy requires the parquet files to give consumer temporary read access to them. It seems in BDC world the onus of generating those parquet files for a virtual Datasphere view is explicit and the engine may not do it on its own. For e.g. in Databricks when a consumer's client queries a shared view, the query is routed to the data provider's Delta Sharing server. The provider's engine (Databricks Unity Catalog) executes the SQL view logic and processes any dynamic access controls (such as row-level filters or column-masking). The engine temporarily stages the resolved view output in the provider's cloud storage (AWS S3, ADLS, or GCS) in a Delta Lake format, maintaining the provider’s governance and permissions. This parquet files are accessed through the temporary url from the consumer.

It could be that in future the parquet equivalent of the resolved view could be automatically generated in HDLFS.

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2605 – What’s New?
entry.content:
The May 2026 release of SAP Business Network for Logistics delivers key innovations across both SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace. These enhancements focus on improving collaboration, increasing transparency in execution, and strengthening system performance and integration reliability.

Below, we highlight the most important innovations following the structure of this release.

 

Cross SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

EDI processing logic enhanced

The EDI processing logic has been improved to ensure smoother and more reliable data exchange. Files that fail due to unrecoverable validation errors—such as incorrect or missing identifiers—are now automatically deleted from the carrier SFTP server. At the same time, processing continues immediately with subsequent valid files, while error details remain available in the ‘Manage Integration Logs’ application.

Business benefit: This enhancement prevents processing bottlenecks and ensures uninterrupted data flow, reducing delays and minimizing manual effort required to resolve integration issues.

Event tracking of gate hold ups completed

A gate hold occurs when a truck has physically arrived at the facility but is prevented from completing the check-in /check-out process to enter the yard or dock area. The holds are typically categorized in Documentation, Scheduling, Operational or Compliance.

With the introduction of the new event type GateOut_Hold (Gate Check-Out on Hold), it is now possible to fully capture and monitor delays at the gate.

The event can be reported in the ‘Manage Gate Operations’ (FC) or ‘Track Shipments’ (GTT) apps (as well as via API).  

 

The events reported via ‘Track Shipments’ application in GTT can be viewed in the tracking timeline and the events tables. A reported gate on hold can set the status of a shipment to ‘In Execution’. Solution owners may adapt this logic in the event to action scripts.

 

The events are also synchronized with the ‘Manage Freight Execution’ app. This ensures that all stakeholders are informed when shipments are delayed due to operational, scheduling, compliance, or documentation issues.

Business benefit: Shippers gain end-to-end visibility into execution delays, enabling faster reaction times, improved planning accuracy, and more transparent communication with logistics partners.

Adaptations in ‘System Connections’ app regarding the sunset of SAP BTP, Neo environment

To support the transition to SAP BTP Multi-Cloud, the Integration Platform dropdown has been removed from the ‘System Connections’ app. All connections now run on the Multi-Cloud environment, and existing carrier inbound connections are automatically migrated.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Enhancement of carrier collaboration options

Carrier collaboration is expanded through EDI-based freight tendering, now supporting ANSI X12 message types 840 (Request for Quotation), 843 (Response), and 864 (Award notification).

This allows carriers to participate in tendering processes choosing between collaboration via Web UI, API or EDI. Message Implementation Guidelines have been published to support carriers.

Business benefit: Organizations can onboard more carriers faster, reduce integration complexity, and enable seamless collaboration using widely adopted EDI standards.

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Instance-based authorization

A new field, ‘OrderingPartyInternalID’, has been introduced in the tracked process, mapped with the parameter ‘InternalID’ and sent to the ’Manage Freight Execution’ app. This supports more granular, instance-based authorization.

Business benefit: This enhancement strengthens data access control and allows organizations to define more precise authorization rules, improving security and governance across logistics processes.

Usability and performance improved in fulfillment tracking apps

Several improvements have been introduced to enhance usability and efficiency across all fulfillment tracking apps. Key fields such as ‘Unplanned Event’, ‘Event Reason Code’, ‘Location Type’, ‘Transportation Means’, and ‘Code List’ now support free text input with intelligent suggestions. So users can quickly find values instead of navigating long dropdown lists.

Business benefit: These improvements significantly reduce the time required for event reporting and increase data entry accuracy, resulting in more efficient operations.

New location update logic to improve performance

The handling of location updates has been redesigned to improve system performance. This results in faster map loading and an overall better experience in tracking applications.

The feature is automatically available for new customers, while existing customers can opt for migration to benefit from the enhanced logic. To do so, create a ticket under the component SBN-LBN-GTT-COR to get migration support. Please note that the migration is irreversible.

Main differences compared to prior map:

  • Map logic based on new GTTGeoUpdateEvent 
  • Map overview mode appears when a shipment has more than 5 tracking units.
  • The current location is shown by an arrow icon for the last reported stop, and when completed it is shown as solid dot. In the legacy text, the current location is described as a flashing dot.
  • The overview shows current positions of tracking units that are still in transit, with a number beside the arrow if several are at the same or nearby location (no completed units anymore)

For details see our Guide for Transportation Planners à Shipment Details à Map (new logic with 2605 release)

Business benefit: Users benefit from faster response times and improved performance when monitoring shipments, especially in high-volume or complex logistics scenarios.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2605. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2605 release can be found under the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: Re: UI5/Fiori Elements V4 Upgrade: Navigating the Address...
entry.content:
Hello @kayur_goyal 

After upgrading to UI5 1.135+, PATCH requests may fail due to long navigation paths being used when this annotation is enabled
<Annotation Term="Common.AddressViaNavigationPath" Bool="true"/>


Disable it at the CDS level to restore old (short path) behavior
@Common.AddressViaNavigationPath: false
entity MyEntity {
// fields
}


This makes PATCH requests use shorter paths again and resolves backend compatibility issues


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Guide to Accessing a Remote Database via Python in Visual Studio
entry.content:
Introduction:
It is possible to connect to a remote PostgreSQL database using Python and then use your application to access and manipulate the data wherever you are. Visual Studio supports writing Python code, and using libraries such as psycopg2 to connect the code to the database easily. This architecture can be effectively used to develop applications that will interoperate with a remote or cloud-hosted database.

Body:

Prerequisites: refer my following blogs
{https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/aiblog-board/article-id/1138?prePageCrumb=BlogDashboardPage}
{https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/aiblog-board/article-id/1139?prePageCrumb=BlogDashboardPage}
{https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/aiblog-board/article-id/1140?prePageCrumb=BlogDashboardPage}

Create environment file .env in VScode

Add the Service URL in it

 

 

Run pip install -r req.txt

 

Go to PostgreSQL Database and Click the Query tool

 

 

Run 

select version() and check the result

 

Go to Visual Studio -> Create file initialize.py ->  Write the following code for the above

That should return the same result received in PostgreSQL admin

import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

# Load our connection URL from .env (private file)
load_dotenv()

try:
    # Connect to the PostgreSQL database using the connection URL
    conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv("DB_URL"))

    query_sql = 'SELECT VERSION()'

    cur = conn.cursor()
    cur.execute(query_sql)

    version = cur.fetchone()[0]
    print(version)

except Exception as e:
    print(f"connection failed: {e}")

finally:
    if 'conn' in locals():
        conn.close()

And thus your application connected to remote PostgreSQL database server


Summary:
To connect, the first step will be to install the libraries that are required and then provide the database connection details i.e. the host, port, username and password. Then, you can create the connection using Python code in Visual Studio and run queries. After connecting, you are able to effectively carry out functions such as reading, inserting, updating, and managing data.

##if you found this blog helpful, please consider giving it a like

entry.title: Re: BP Grouping : Telephone number Mandatory
entry.content:
Hi SwamyNaidu,

first of all I would like to remind you to some communication rules at SAP Community.
This community is no chat bot!
If you start with a short 'Hello' and give some more context, will help to keep the kind of communication within this community high as it is.

But now to you question:
The only way I can see is to use your own Function Module at Field Group 72 in transaction BUS2. With active CVI field control of account group is considered but unfortunately at account group level phone number, fax number and telex is bound into one field group. In this case you have to use field control on BP side.

May be the way described in blog post SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Implement Custom Field Check is an option. 

If you are interested in field status, have a look into blog post SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Field Status Analysis
Best Regards
Andi

entry.title: F110 Payment Work flow approval request not triggered to the two agents
entry.content:
Is is required to set up two workflow agents to approve a payment in F110 payment run approver workflow.

Standard work flow template - WS23200018 

Two agents were defined in Agent determination for the said payment amount range.

The 2 approvers do get the workflow request to approve the payment proposal, however, after one approver approves the request it passes the whole workflow to proceed to payment even without the approval from the second approver.

Could you please advice how to resolve this.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Requisition generated for non-finalized Sale...
entry.content: By default, PRs are generated immediately when saving a sales order with external procurement. To delay until “finalization,” you need to implement custom logic tied to sales order status or use a workflow approval step. This ensures PRs are only created once the order is truly confirmed/sent, aligning with your automatic PR approval workflow.

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS View how to display fields as properties for...
entry.content:
Hello @filomena_melardo_1997 

SAC won’t pick attributes from associations automatically.
You must flatten the fields in the CUBE and mark them as attributes:

@ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ZAttivitaAttr'
base./bic/z_attivit as /bic/zAttivit,

@AnalyticsDetails.attribute: true
_ZAttivitaAttr./bic/zMambito as ZMambito,

@AnalyticsDetails.attribute: true
_ZAttivitaAttr./bic/zAmbito as ZAmbito


Explicitly selected in the cube
Annotated with @AnalyticsDetails.attribute: true


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Withholding data not populating for 26Q report in the...
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

Please note, data (Challan details) is populating based upon the reporting period did not based upon the due date for 26Q report. If the challan made for the particular month, then challan documents will be reported to the particular quarter.

 

Ex: If you are reporting to the 1st quarter (01/01/2026 - 31/03/2026) and you have posted the document made the challan after 1st quarter (Ex: month of April) then the challan documents will be reports to the 2nd quarter (1st quarter of the April).

 

Note: You can download the legal file in TXT format and submit it to the tax authorities by manually uploading it to their official portal before the prescribed due date. Although the reporting is done on a quarterly basis, the tax deducted at source (TDS) must be remitted to the government on a monthly basis, within 7 days from the end of each month.

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: UK Postponed Import VAT Accounting in SAP
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

I am looking for guidance from anyone with UK VAT / HMRC experience in SAP, specifically regarding the correct accounting treatment for monthly Postponed Import VAT (PIVA).

Background:
Each month our customer receive a postponed import VAT statement from HMRC. In the previous system, they could simply upload the statement into the VAT return module and manually enter the figures. The system automatically created the necessary contra postings between input VAT and output VAT.

In SAP, however, this process does not appear to be straightforward.

What we are trying to achieve:
We need to post a VAT-only document that correctly updates both:

  • Input VAT G/L account
  • Output VAT G/L account

without impacting supplier balances or net expense values.

What we have researched so far:
One suggested approach was to create a VAT-only supplier invoice using a VF tax code (or similar configuration involving tax-only postings / reverse charge logic).

We tested this in our SAP test system, but we are encountering configuration-related errors when attempting multiple assignment lines for the tax code. It appears SAP may require additional tax code configuration to allow this process.

Questions:

  1. How are UK companies typically handling postponed import VAT accounting in SAP?
  2. Is there a standard SAP process for HMRC PIVA postings?

Thank you very much in advance for any guidance.

Best regards

Vanessa

#SAP #SAPFI #UKVAT #PostponedImportVAT #S4HANA #DRC

entry.title: Re: How to derive the Sold-to Party on COGS Split posting...
entry.content:
You are correct in your observations:

1) KEDR (Account-Based CO‑PA Derivation)
A table lookup on VBAK can technically work if the sales order reference (KAUFN/VBELN) is reliably available in ACDOCA at the time of COGS posting. However, in intercompany flows, the delivering company’s FI document may not carry the selling company’s sales order number in a way that KEDR can consistently resolve. This is why your lookup may not always populate the correct sold‑to party. If you want to enforce this at CO‑PA level, KEDR or F4406 (derivation tool for Margin Analysis) is the right place, not FI substitution.

2) FI Substitution (GGB1)
Your understanding is correct. GGB1 operates on BSEG/FI line items and cannot directly modify CO‑PA characteristics in ACDOCA. Therefore, substitution at FI level will not solve the reporting issue in Margin Analysis. The correct approach is to derive the customer characteristic at CO‑PA level (KEDR/F4406).

3) FINS_ACDOC_REPOST (Realignment)
For historical data, FINS_ACDOC_REPOST is indeed the recommended tool to realign CO‑PA characteristics. It allows you to retroactively adjust customer characteristics in ACDOCA without changing the original FI postings. This ensures consistency between FI and CO‑PA ledgers, because FI remains untouched while CO‑PA characteristics are corrected. The risk of inconsistency is minimal if you use FINS_ACDOC_REPOST, since it is designed for retroactive realignment in Margin Analysis.
